fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Providing clear rear access to the main building, possibly through reduced or managed vegetation, may enhance emergency responder access and operational efficiency. Ensuring that dense vegetation is well-maintained and trimmed back could facilitate more effective responder movement and improve visibility during emergency responses. Creating a dedicated pathway through any existing spaces or landscaping to improve rear entry may assist responders in navigating the property more efficiently and could support better staging and operational tactics in various scenarios. These adjustments would likely aid in maintaining concise lines of sight and swift access to critical areas for emergency scenarios.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Structure Surrounded by Vegetation; Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetation Overhang; Vegetated Perimeter; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route
wildfire.recommendations: Improving the defensible space around the property could benefit from increasing the separation between vegetation and the structure, particularly where vegetation is adjacent to the building. This action may help reduce the continuity of fuel that currently surrounds the structure. Thinning and managing the dense tree canopy, as well as minimizing vegetation overhang near the structure, could also reduce potential ignition sources and flame exposure. Addressing the tree cluster and scattered trees by ensuring there is adequate distance between them and other vegetation may help further reduce fuel continuity. Maintaining areas of reduced vegetation along the perimeter and near the access route can enhance firefighter access and facilitate suppression efforts. Consistent management of vegetation along the driveway and access routes would support access and provide safer entrance and exit routes, contributing to the long-term resilience of the property against wildfires.
